channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: No. 2 Georgia is still the top dog in the SEC after convincing win over previously top-ranked Texas
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas and everyone else are finding that Georgia is still the top dog in the Southeastern Conference. Coach Kirby Smart's 100th victory in his nine seasons with the Bulldogs, a dominating defensive performance at No.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cooper scores in his Bills debut and Allen shakes off a sluggish start in 34-10 rout of Titans
item.description: Amari Cooper scored the go-ahead touchdown in his debut with Buffalo and Josh Allen overcame a sluggish first half to throw two touchdown passes in his 100th career start to help the Bills rally from a 10-point deficit and beat the Tennessee Titans 34-10.
item.dc:creator: John Wawrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lions drill field goal in final seconds to hand Vikings first loss of season
item.description: The Detroit Lions blew their double-digit lead to the Minnesota Vikings, but were able to kick a field goal in the final seconds to come away with another victory.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Browns QB Deshaun Watson is carted off the field with a right Achilles tendon injury
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Deshaun Watson's unsuccessful and disappointing stay with Cleveland has taken another significant turn. The embattled quarterback was carted off the field Sunday with a right Achilles tendon injury he suffered on a noncontact play against the Cincinnati Bengals.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Steve Sarkisian explains why he temporarily benched Quinn Ewers for Arch Manning
item.description: For the first time since they were teammates, Quinn Ewers was benched in favor of Arch Manning – although temporary, it prompted some questions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Juan Soto's 3-run homer in 10th sends Yankees past Guardians 5-2 and into World Series for 41st time
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Juan Soto was supposed to be the difference-maker, the one who pushed the New York Yankees back to the top. They're one step away.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Haason Reddick is ending his contract holdout and plans to report to the Jets on Monday
item.description: Haason Reddick is ending his long holdout with the New York Jets and will report to the team on Monday. Agent Drew Rosenhaus told The Associated Press on Sunday that Reddick agreed to an adjusted contract for this season and the sides will continue to work toward a long-term deal beyond this year.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 5 Georgia knocks off No. 1 Texas 30-15, with Etienne running for 3 TDs
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Trevor Etienne ran for three touchdowns, the first two set up by cornerback Daylen Everette's takeaways, and fifth-ranked Georgia went on to beat Quinn Ewers and No. 1 Texas 30-15 on Saturday night. Etienne's last score was a 1-yard plunge on fourth down with 12:04 left.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Saquon Barkley returns to MetLife — with the Eagles
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Former Giants running back Saquon Barkley will step back inside MetLife Stadium on Sunday as a member of the Eagles. Newsday Giants beat reporter Kim Jones joins New York Blitz to discuss what to expect from fans and why Barkley wasn’t a Giant “for life.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: SDSU'S Sack Master Trey White
item.description: Sacks are such an unpredictable stat in the game of Football. You can be absolutely unlockable as a defender, but if the quarterback gets the ball out of his hands before you arrive, it's all for not in the stat column.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Real Salt Lake legend reflects on his journey, leadership, and life beyond the pitch
item.description: Real Salt Lake legend and current Vancouver Whitecaps FC standout Damir Kreilach joins Royal Riot co-host Mattey Casey for an exclusive interview, just ahead of his much-anticipated return to America First Field.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, ABC 4

item.title: Former NFL quarterback Jay Cutler arrested on DUI, gun charges
item.description: The former Bears and Broncos quarterback Jwas arrested Thursday in Tennessee for allegedly driving intoxicated.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, Fox 5

item.title: First on PIX: NY Liberty's playoff run generates $18 million for NYC
item.description: BROOKLYN, N.Y. (PIX11) -- The New York Liberty's WNBA playoff run, including the ongoing championship series, has generated $18.3 million in economic impact for New York City.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: GJ Tigers soccer downs Central, inches closer to league title
item.description: In a big southwestern league soccer showdown at Community Hospital Unity Field, the Grand Junction Tigers stayed hot.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Cole Kmet earns NFC Special Teams Player of the Week honors
item.description: Cole Kmet was named NFC Special Teams Player of the Week for Week 6 after successfully snapping on all five of the Bears' point-after attempts during their game in London against the Jacksonville Jaguars.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Clayton Kershaw planning to pitch again next season
item.description: Clayton Kershaw says he plans to return to the Los Angeles Dodgers for his 18th season next year. The three-time NL Cy Young Award winner isn’t pitching in the current playoffs because of a bone spur in his left big toe that sidelined him for the final month of the regular season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Aidan Hutchinson likely down 4-6 months with broken leg, Lions coach Dan Campbell says
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Aidan Hutchinson is likely done playing this year because of the leg injury he sustained against the Cowboys, but surgery to repair his fractured tibia and fibula “went great” and Lions coach Dan Campbell said Monday he isn't ready to declare his star pass rusher out for the
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pro rider Cam Zink breaks ribs in hard fall during Red Bull Rampage mountain biking competition
item.description: Pro mountain biker Cameron Zink suffered six broken ribs and a punctured lung after a hard crash at Red Bull Rampage -- a particularly challenging freeride competition -- in southern Utah on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Jerry West becomes first three-time inductee as Carter and Billups enter basketball's Hall of Fame
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Mass. (AP) — The stage was filled with Laker greats when Jerry West made history Sunday night by becoming the first three-time inductee to the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tampa Bay Lightning donates tickets to first responders; offers discounted tickets for first 3 home games
item.description: The Tampa Bay Lightning donated tickets to hundreds of first responders, other recovery workers, and their families following the aftermath of Hurricane Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Matt McCarty wins first PGA event at Black Desert Championship
item.description: IVINS, Utah (AP) - IVINS, Utah (AP) — Matt McCarty started the year on the Korn Ferry Tour and now is headed to the Masters, winning his first PGA Tour title Sunday at the Black Desert Championship with a 4-under 67 and one swing that all but sealed it.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Dodgers tie postseason mark of 33 straight scoreless innings, top Mets 9-0 in NLCS opener
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — After spending the first seven years of his big league career back East, Jack Flaherty came home. He joined a winning team in the Los Angeles Dodgers and helped make a bit of playoff history.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Doubs catches 2 TD passes in his return from a suspension to help Packers rout Cardinals 34-13
item.description: Romeo Doubs wanted to make amends after serving a one-game suspension for what the Green Bay Packers described as conduct detrimental to the team.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, Fox 5
